steps.text: Provide operational oversight and advice to the Assistant Secretary, program directors, and regional administrators on compliance with this instruction and related processes.
Manage the OSHA directive system, including dissemination of policy and non-policy issuances.
Oversee the administration of the program and provide training in its operation.
Oversee program administration and provide operational training.
As the Agency Administrative Officer, signs all policy, procedural issuances, and national directives pertaining to administrative matters.
Effectively implement and comply with provisions of this and related instructions.
Assess and routinely monitor program performance compliance.
Responsible for the review and assessment of their active directives on an annual basis with a final document of the review findings at the end of each fiscal year.
Initiate and develop proposed NPIs within the scope of their authority or significantly affecting the operations of their programs.

Based on the subject and intended application of a proposed NPI, request review and clearance by appropriate directorates, regional offices, Office of the Solicitor (SOL), the Assistant Secretary, etc.
Provide proposed NPI and necessary background information to ensure the proposed issuance is thoroughly understood and considered.
Establish a due date for response return, allowing no more than three weeks turnaround time.
Review and consider comments received and address them as appropriate.

Refer to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary any unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the substance or potential policy implications of the proposed NPI or to the priority accorded to the proposed NPI’s review that emerged during the clearance process.
Ensure that systems for receiving, reviewing, tracking, concurring, and issuing NPIs are effective for consistently complying with requirements of this instruction.
Complete items 1 through 8 on the OSHA 201.
Determine whether field coordination is necessary.
Allow a minimum of 15 working days for review and clearance. Consult with the directives officer in the event a shorter clearance period is desired.
Prepare an electronic version of the OSHA 201 and a draft directive to provide electronically to all clearance officials and proxies.
Identify all clearance officials and proxies who will receive a copy.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director and send copies to the clearance officials and proxies.
Review and reconcile any comments noted in item 8 of the OSHA 201 by clearance officials/proxies in the National Office and in the field.
Contact nonresponsive clearance offices prior to the review deadline to determine clearance/comments submission and offer an opportunity to discuss any questions or issues.
Review the draft directive. If necessary, request an extension from the originator as soon as possible.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator. Alternatively, sends concurrence or comments electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.).
Negotiate the acceptance of comments with the originator or originating Program Director or presents the case to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate for approval clearance.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and requests the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Coordinate union review through the Labor Management Relations Officer.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the union, delayed posting request, or pending press release.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file directive to the web administrator for posting to the Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage within the requested timeframe.
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.

Before a direction may be drafted, the originator will convene a meeting with directorates and regional offices to provide information on the content of the direction and provide an opportunity for discussion of initial questions and issues related to the content of the direction, to focus and expedite review by identifying and addressing potential issues and problems as soon as possible.

Before a direction may go into effect, the originator must provide a copy of the proposed direction to each clearance office required to provide clearance if the direction was issued as an instruction. Clearance offices have up to five working days from the date of the OSHA 201 to consider the proposed direction and provide comments.

If a resolution is unable to be reached, outstanding issue(s) are elevated to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary, and as necessary, to the Assistant Secretary, for a decision regarding the issue of contention.
Prepare the direction clearance packet.
Submit the packet, along with a request for approval and signature of the Assistant Secretary, to the directives officer for processing.
Provide a 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer upon approval.
Coordinate the publication of the direction with the web administrator.
Review the direction six months after issuance to determine if it will be continued or replaced.
Contact clearance official if their office fails to return the OSHA 201 by the deadline to negotiate an extension or resolve issues.
Schedule a meeting with the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary if there is a failure to achieve an agreement on comments from clearance offices.
Elevate any outstanding issues to the Assistant Secretary for a decision.
Prepare a cancellation notice using the cancellation template on the OSHA Directives Tools webpage.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director.
Provide the cancellation notice and concurrence grid providing review by the originating directorate, the directives officer, and the OAS.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting to the directives webpage.
Review the cancellation packet to determine if complete and accurate for the approval process.
Provide revision request from the OAS to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and request the cancellation notice be formatted to 508-compliant PDF.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the Assistant Secretary.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file to the web administrator for posting to the OSHA Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF to OSHA Directives webpage within requested timeframe.
